message: Add search query execution with filter support Implement query module providing filtered full-text search over Tantivy index with support for agent filtering, workspace filtering, and time range filtering. SearchFilters struct (query parameters): Encapsulates all supported filter criteria for narrowing search results: - agents: HashSet of agent slugs to include (e.g., {"codex", "cline"}), empty set = no agent filter (match all agents) - workspace: Option for exact workspace path match (e.g., Some("/home/user/projects/foo")), None = no workspace filter - started_after: Option unix millis timestamp for lower bound on message created_at (e.g., Some(1700000000000) = messages after 2023-11-15), None = no lower bound - started_before: Option for upper bound on created_at, None = no upper bound Derives Default for convenience (SearchFilters::default() = no filters, match all documents). Derives Clone for passing to async tasks. Derives Debug for logging filter values in search debugging. HashSet for agents: Use HashSet vs Vec because: - Deduplication: User might specify same agent twice via UI, set automatically deduplicates (clicking "Codex" checkbox twice doesn't create duplicate filter) - Lookup efficiency: O(1) contains check if needed (not currently used, but supports future "is agent enabled" queries) - Semantic clarity: Set conveys "unique collection" intent vs Vec's "ordered sequence" SearchResult struct (output): Wrapper around Tantivy's (f32, DocAddress) tuple plus retrieved Document: - doc: tantivy::Document with all STORED fields (agent, workspace, source_path, msg_idx, created_at, title, content per schema) - score: f32 BM25 relevance score (higher = better match, typical range 0.0-20.0 for short queries, can exceed 50.0 for long multi-term queries with many field matches) - address: tantivy::DocAddress identifying document location (segment_ord, doc_id within segment) for future operations (delete, update, explain score breakdown) Returning Document in SearchResult: Eagerly retrieve doc via searcher.doc(addr) during search rather than lazily on-demand because: - UI needs doc fields immediately (extract title, content snippet for results list display) - Batch retrieval more efficient (single segment scan vs multiple round-trips if UI fetches docs one-by-one) - Error handling simplified (search fails fast if doc retrieval errors, vs UI handling partial results where some docs missing) execute function (main API): Signature: fn execute(index: &TantivyIndex, query: &str, filters: SearchFilters, limit: usize) -> Result> Parameters: - index: Reference to TantivyIndex (created by indexer, held in TUI state or passed via channel) - query: User's search string (e.g., "api refactor", "error handling"), empty string allowed (returns all docs matching filters, sorted by created_at descending) - filters: SearchFilters struct with optional agent/workspace/time constraints - limit: Max results to return (UI passes 100 per PLAN § 5.3, prevents memory exhaustion if query matches millions of docs) Returns: - Vec sorted by BM25 score descending (best matches first), truncated to limit - Empty vec if no matches (not an error, UI displays "No results found") - Err(TantivyError) if query parsing fails (invalid syntax like unclosed quote "api) or index read fails (segments corrupted) Query building strategy (BooleanQuery composition): Construct tantivy::query::BooleanQuery with multiple clauses combined via Occur enum: - Occur::Must: Document must match this clause (AND logic) - Occur::Should: Document should match at least one Should clause (OR logic within Should group) - Occur::MustNot: Document must not match (NOT logic, exclusion) Clause assembly logic: 1. Text query (if non-empty): - Parse user query string with QueryParser::for_index targeting title and content fields (multi-field search per PLAN § 5.3) - QueryParser handles phrase queries ("api refactor" matches docs with adjacent words), boolean operators (foo AND bar), field syntax (title:api), wildcards (refactor*) - Add as Occur::Must clause (documents must match text query) 2. Agent filter (if agents non-empty): - For each agent slug in set, create TermQuery matching exact agent field value (Term::from_field_text creates term for indexed field) - Combine agent TermQueries with BooleanQuery using Occur::Should (match any agent in set, OR logic: codex OR cline) - Add agent BooleanQuery as Occur::Must clause to outer query (documents must match at least one agent in filter) 3. Workspace filter (if Some): - Create TermQuery for exact workspace field match (Term::from_field_text with workspace path string) - Add as Occur::Must clause (documents must match workspace) 4. Time range filter (if either bound present): - Construct RangeQuery::new_i64 with created_at field and inclusive range (lower..=upper) - Use i64::MIN for missing lower bound (match all docs before upper) - Use i64::MAX for missing upper bound (match all docs after lower) - Add as Occur::Must clause (documents must have timestamp in range) Empty query handling: If clauses vec is empty after filter assembly (no text query, no filters), construct fallback QueryParser with content field only (prevents BooleanQuery::new panic on empty clauses). This case occurs when: - User hasn't typed search text yet (UI shows all conversations by default) - User cleared all filters (reset to show everything) Fallback query matches nothing (empty parser, no query string), returning empty results (correct behavior: if no criteria specified, show nothing vs showing everything which would overwhelm UI). Search execution: 1. Create IndexReader from index (reader.searcher() for query execution) 2. Execute BooleanQuery with TopDocs collector limiting to specified limit (TopDocs::with_limit internally uses binary heap to track top-K results without sorting all matches) 3. Iterate top_docs (Vec<(f32, DocAddress)>) to build SearchResult vec: - For each (score, address), call searcher.doc(address) to retrieve full Document - Wrap in SearchResult struct with score and address 4. Return results vec (already sorted by score descending from TopDocs collector) BM25 scoring details: Tantivy's QueryParser uses BM25 algorithm for relevance ranking: - Term frequency (TF): Words appearing multiple times in doc boost score - Inverse document frequency (IDF): Rare words (appearing in few docs) boost score more than common words (appearing in many docs) - Field length normalization: Short docs with query match score higher than long docs with same match (penalizes verbosity) - Multi-field boosting: Title matches weighted 3x vs content matches (not implemented yet, requires custom Boost query wrapper per PLAN § 5.3 "title weight 3.0, content weight 1.0") Error propagation: All Tantivy operations return Result, propagated via ? operator: - index.reader()?: Index corruption, segments unreadable - qp.parse_query(query)?: Invalid query syntax, unclosed quotes - searcher.search(...)?: Query execution failure (OOM, segment I/O error) - searcher.doc(addr)?: Document retrieval failure (segment deleted mid-search, rare but possible during compaction) Integration with TUI (future): TUI search input handler will: 1. Debounce keystrokes (~150ms) to avoid search-on-every-key 2. Build SearchFilters from UI state (selected agent checkboxes, workspace dropdown, date range picker) 3. Call execute with user's query string, filters, limit=100 4. Render results in left pane list widget (extract title, created_at, agent from SearchResult.doc for display) 5. On row selection, extract source_path and msg_idx from doc, query SQLite for full conversation with messages ordered by idx Testing strategy (manual): ```rust let index = TantivyIndex::open_or_create(path)?; let filters = SearchFilters { agents: ["codex"].iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect(), ..Default::default() }; let results = execute(&index, "api", filters, 10)?; for r in results { println!("score={} title={:?}", r.score, r.doc.get_first(title_field)); } ``` Known limitations (future enhancements): - No field weighting (title should score 3x content per PLAN § 5.3, requires Boost query wrapper) - No snippet highlighting (results don't include matched text, requires tantivy::snippet::SnippetGenerator) - No fuzzy matching (typos don't match, "refactr" won't find "refactor", requires FuzzyTermQuery with edit distance 1-2) - No pagination (limit is hard cap, no offset parameter for "next page", requires custom TopDocs collector tracking offset) - No aggregations (can't group results by agent, count messages per workspace, requires custom Collector implementation) Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 5.3 (query model with filters), § 5.4 (performance targets <50ms), search/tantivy.rs (TantivyIndex struct, pub Fields), future TUI commit (search input widget calling execute). 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) Co-Authored-By: Claude
